name: T-Bone Walker
description: American blues guitarist, singer, songwriter and multi-instrumentalist
abstract: Aaron Thibeaux "T-Bone" Walker (May 28, 1910 – March 16, 1975) was an American blues musician, composer, songwriter, and bandleader, who was a pioneer and innovator of the jump blues, West Coast blues, and electric blues sounds. In 2018  Rolling Stone magazine ranked him No.

works.t: Call It Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad)

concerts.label: Blues Variations: Albert King, T-Bone Walker, and Climax Blues Band

works_created.t: Call It Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad)

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: I tell you what, back then I was influenced by Roy Brown, T-Bone Walker, and mostly Roy Brown

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: She has accompanied Joe Williams, Big Mama Thornton and T-Bone Walker
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: I played with T-Bone back in the days when...at the Plantation... T-Bone and I used to ride down there... by the time you came to Europe in '68 with T-Bone

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Simmons
about_quotes.evidence: I played for T-Bone Walker, you know what to mean? I had a gig with him and stuff like this, within a jazz band, not in a blues band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 27, 2007 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call to go to France to make a five day tour with Jo Jones, Milt Buckner, and T-Bone Walker.

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: You did make a record with T-Bone Walker, didn't you, then? That is true, we sure did.
